data.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay)

The overlay applies: the plan defines part of an LLM/agent review system (a REVIEW_RESULT output schema, a per-criterion verdict vocabulary, finding fingerprints, and references to two sibling agents — orchestrator `ca03` and Pass-4 coach `75a9`). I tool-grounded the referenced agents/enums/schema/payload. One proven structural gap; the rest of the T8 surface is clean.

FINDING
- [ ] Define the per-pass LATENCY + COST metadata shape the added AC requires — schema location, value vocabulary, units, and per-pass keying.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: Plan §"Acceptance Criteria (added — close G3 coverage)", AC "Capture per-pass LATENCY + COST … in the sidecar metadata"
  - evidence: The AC names a metadata field ("per-pass LATENCY + COST", distinguishing "the fast claim-path check" vs "the out-of-band review") but never defines its shape. The implemented sidecar payload (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:68-82`) carries `schema/verdict/ticket_id/ticket_type/material_fingerprint/model/runner/coverage/findings/coaching` — no latency or cost field. `coverage` in the verdict schema is a free-form object (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json:46-50`, `additionalProperties: true`) describing only "what ran" (DET coverage, routing, chunk count, llm_ran, verdict counts). An executor cannot tell WHERE these metrics live (a new top-level `metrics` field? a `coverage.latency`/`coverage.cost` sub-key?), the UNITS (wall-clock ms? input/output tokens? USD?), or how the two passes are KEYED so the offline join can separate the fast claim-path check from the out-of-band review. This is the T8 pattern — a metadata vocabulary referenced by an AC but never specified — so the "refined PASSIVELY from dogfood data" promise has no defined substrate.
  - suggested_fix: Add a `metrics` (or `coverage.metrics`) object to the sidecar payload schema with explicit per-pass keys and units, e.g. `{"pass": "det-floor"|"pass1-find"|"pass2-verify"|"pass3-decide"|"pass4-coach"|"claim-path-check", "wall_ms": int, "tokens": {"in": int, "out": int}, "usd": float|null}`, and state that the fast claim-path check and the out-of-band review are recorded as distinct keyed entries so offline analysis can separate them.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk passes)
- No undefined-but-referenced enum: the per-criterion VERDICT vocabulary the sidecar records is fully defined — `verdict` is PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json:10-13`), and the per-finding fields the sidecar slims (`id`, `criteria`, `tier`, `decision`, `severity`, `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `reason`, `verification`) are enumerated in `sidecar.py:47-59`. "finding fingerprints" resolves to the content-fingerprint `id` minted by `orchestrator.mint_finding_id` (`ca03`).
- Referenced agents exist and are fully specified (tool-verified): orchestrator `ca03` (dry-frost-zenith, CLOSED) is the SOLE owner that MINTS the finding `id`; Pass-4 coach `75a9` (crusty-herb-stand) CONSUMES it via `finding_refs`; the sidecar RECORDS it. The id-ownership protocol is unambiguous and non-circular (mint-once/consume) — matching `ca03`'s What and `75a9`'s "Caller-visible finding id contract". No pink-elephant / instruction-locality antipattern: the sidecar records the id and does not re-mint it.
- No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement: idempotency placement is explicitly disambiguated — the sidecar is APPEND-ONLY per run (NOT idempotent, by design), while idempotency lives on the ATTESTATION head-state (one current valid signature). Exactly the kind of state-placement spec T8 looks for, and it is present.
- A fallback for a failed sub-step is specified: the sidecar emit is best-effort and "a failed emit must NEVER fail the review itself" — the observability substrate degrades without taxing the hot path.
- The rollout/coexistence protocol referenced by the added AC is real and co-located with the schema: `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` includes `REVIEW_RESULT` and `is_unknown_newer_type` distinguishes truly-newer types (`reducer/_version.py`); fsck WARNs on newer types; preserve-and-ignore + readers-before-writers documented at `sidecar.py:7-14`. No structural gap.

Net: one MAJOR-candidate structural gap (undefined latency/cost metadata vocabulary on a referenced schema field); the rest of the T8 surface is clean.
